refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for decision-making.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure update latency statistics (p95), validate data accuracy through comparison with ground truth or sensor data, and verify continuous availability during operational scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process sensor and telemetry data from vehicles and site equipment within 0.5 s.
FR2: Integrate processed data into the centralized worksite model ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time data ingestion pipeline with prioritized telemetry processing.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with incremental update algorithms.
DP3: Event-driven notification system for worksite model distribution.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to meet latency and accuracy requirements.

potential_sources: Data synchronization between ingestion pipeline and centralized database.
Notification timing coupling with data update completion.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interfaces and asynchronous event handling to minimize tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency is within acceptable bounds to support p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
Risk of data loss or delay due to network congestion or hardware failure.
Assumes telemetry data quality is sufficient for accurate model updates.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and implement fallback mechanisms to handle data delays or losses.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data ingestion, processing, model updating, and distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and vehicle telemetry data.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data communication between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data and commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary containment of all data processing and model update logic.
Receives updates but does not contain update logic.
Consumes updated model data for operator interface.
Receives safety alerts but does not process model updates.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update logic within NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports tight latency requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded.
Single point of failure risk for worksite model updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or modularizing NGHE Solution internally to mitigate bottlenecks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly maps to selected DPs with measurable verification criteria and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: High diffusion risk if update logic is spread across multiple modules; currently centralized to mitigate.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end within NGHE Solution using telemetry and network interface monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update latency responsibility to NGHE Solution with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor NGHE Solution module load and consider internal modularization.
Validate network link performance assumptions during integration.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the latency and safety criticality requirements with clear verification paths and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process sensor and telemetry data from vehicles and site equipment within 0.5 s.
FR2: Integrate processed data into the centralized worksite model ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; next step is to decompose sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network link latency and reliability meet operational requirements.
Telemetry data quality is sufficient for accurate model updates.
open_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module under high load.
Single point of failure risk for centralized update logic.
evidence_needed: Network latency and throughput measurements under operational conditions.
Load testing results for NGHE Solution processing capabilities.
